model: Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology (UTAUT)
citation: Venkatesh, V., Morris, M. G., Davis, G. B., & Davis, F. D. (2003). User acceptance of information technology: Toward a unified view. MIS Quarterly, 27(3), 425–478.
application: User acceptance evaluation of OSAS Web-Based Student Management System (WBSMS) at Legacy College of Compostela

constructs.name: Performance Expectancy
constructs.definition: The degree to which the user believes that using the system will help them achieve gains in job performance.
constructs.measurement_focus: Usefulness, efficiency, productivity improvement in OSAS tasks

constructs.name: Effort Expectancy
constructs.definition: The degree of ease associated with the use of the system.
constructs.measurement_focus: Ease of use, learnability, clarity of interface

constructs.name: Social Influence
constructs.definition: The degree to which the user perceives that important others believe they should use the system.
constructs.measurement_focus: Peer influence, supervisory support, institutional norms

constructs.name: Facilitating Conditions
constructs.definition: The degree to which the user believes that organizational and technical infrastructure exists to support system use.

constructs.name: Behavioral Intention
constructs.definition: The extent to which users intend to use the system in the future.
constructs.measurement_focus: Willingness to recommend, continued use intention

hypotheses.text: Performance Expectancy is positively correlated with Behavioral Intention.
Effort Expectancy is positively correlated with Behavioral Intention.
Social Influence is positively correlated with Behavioral Intention.
Facilitating Conditions is positively correlated with Behavioral Intention.
